transcript.clean_text: well there are a few constants when it comes to tyranno adriatico and certainly this year it was blue skies very low temperatures and a dominant performance by telepocatcha today san bernardino our starting point indeed the finishing point for the great race well we headed out into the hills before four laps to glory of course for the sprinters daddy picacho would be in the service of pascal ackerman on the final day and in fact the owner of all of the jerseys except the mountains jersey was ready for action he looked calm and collected at the start quinn simmons in green the king of the mountains the american for trek sega freighter good race for them their hopes resting on the shoulders of matteo mos getty well if it was about the sprinters plenty of absentees of course with caleb ewan not here viviani likewise magnus court getting tangled up in a crash in the morning and a collarbone injury i'm afraid taking him out as well well of course was also involved with that but rode on and in fact gumbo vismo were part of the pursuit likewise honor demar's group armor francis de gea they looked lively holding in abeyance our breakaway whose maximum gap was never more than four minutes well they took the only climb of the day but it was of no consequence and they were to roll through and take the intermediate sprint as well and so as the laps counted down there were to be four in total we were ready for action and also having a preview of some sketchy corners between three kilometers to go and only unwinding around about the 800 meters to go marker slowly but surely the breakaway were found out first to back into the pack tonelli from bardiani our cost from moviestar and buarro from astana were soon to follow and so it's color order for the sprinters teams would they get through the gaps safely that was the question well they took the bell but it was only after a real sketchy pinch at this point very narrow that's the flam rouge just ahead of them and this was a warning to all teams to take it sensibly for the last time of asking well the bell tolled on all the breakaway was done and dusted and the ambition was being shown by bahrain victorious who'd be working for phil bauhaus had a great race with uh michael lander managing a podium but some huge sprinters names in fact winners in the past edward bozenhagen up towards the four on a day that would surely suit alexander kristoff from intermarche wanti despite the bumps and bruises for his team big right-hander just underneath the three kilometers to go marker announcing a sequence of curves that would ultimately lead to the big old pinch around about the flam rouge they all got through safely led very well by israel premier tech working for giacomo nitzola it was busy particularly over there in the sunny side of the road the big move the man in red was phil bauhaus however couldn't find space and timed it beautifully moved over to the opposite side of the road and got himself a well-earned win some big names were booked on the approach mark cavanish just uh tangling with badiani's fiarelli anna dimaggio squeezed into the barriers by maya charging alexander kristoff all's fair they say in love war and sprinting phil bauhaus triumphant and the raw said it all great result for him bow has taken it ahead of hit solo groves jim ally dainesy christoph bozenhagen koi damar and moschetti not perhaps the names that you would have thought would be there merlion cavenish amongst those missing out the blue jersey though went to telly paga we knew it would be upon his back he also owns the points jersey and the young riders classification what an amazing race and what an amazing winner lander and bingo go sharing the podium but the top step belonging to teddy boccaccia we'll be saying that quite a few times this year i imagine every race he's contested so far he has one what more to come neptune's trident belongs to him fantastic race a worthy winner tereno it never disappoints and this year it was special
